label: Asana
description: Connect to Asana to read and manage projects, tasks, and team workspaces.

form.title: Connect Asana
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and manage your Asana projects and tasks.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a Personal Access Token (PAT) generated from your Asana developer settings. No OAuth setup required — the fastest way to connect.
methods.how_to:
## Generate an Asana Personal Access Token

Personal Access Tokens give Anton full API access scoped to your Asana account. They're the simplest way to connect without any OAuth client setup.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Asana](https://app.asana.com) and click your **profile photo** in the top-right corner.
2. Select **My Settings** from the dropdown.
3. Switch to the **Apps** tab.
4. Click **Manage Developer Apps** — this opens the developer console.
5. Under **Personal access tokens**, click **New access token**.
6. Give the token a name like `Anton` and click **Create token**.
7. Copy the token shown — **it is only displayed once**.
8. Paste the token into the **Personal Access Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token inherits the permissions of your Asana account — it can read and write any workspace, project, or task you have access to.
- If you lose the token, revoke it in the same panel and generate a new one.
- Tokens do not expire by default, but a Workspace admin can enforce expiry policies.
- Read [Asana's PAT guide](https://developers.asana.com/docs/personal-access-token) for full details.

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Found in Asana → My Settings → Apps → Manage Developer Apps → Personal access tokens.

fields.name: workspace_gid
fields.label: Workspace GID (optional)
fields.description: Optional. If your account belongs to multiple workspaces, provide the numeric GID of the one you want Anton to default to. Leave blank to use your primary workspace.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Connect via Asana's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own Asana app credentials. Suitable for teams or automated setups where a personal token isn't appropriate.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Asana OAuth 2.0

This method requires you to register an app in Asana's developer console and supply a Client ID and Client Secret. Anton then opens Asana's consent screen in your browser.

### Steps

1. Go to [Asana Developer Console](https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps) and click **+ Create new app**.
2. Fill in an app name (e.g. `Anton`) and select your default workspace.
3. Under **OAuth**, set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect for the desktop flow).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** from the app's details page.
5. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window opens to Asana's authorization screen. Log in and click **Allow**.
7. Anton completes the token exchange and stores the refresh token locally.

### Notes

- The `default` scope grants access to all Asana data your account can reach.
- You can revoke access at any time from **My Settings → Apps → Authorized apps** in Asana.
- Read [Asana's OAuth guide](https://developers.asana.com/docs/oauth) for full details.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From Asana Developer Console → your app → OAuth section.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; found on the same app details page in the developer console.
